factors.summary: Demand is decelerating due to lower sales volumes in multiple segments.

evidence.text: The 1.1 percent decrease in net sales in the first quarter of 2024 from 2023 was primarily driven by decreased sales volume in our residential flow business within our Flow segment and our residential business within our Water Solutions segment compared to the same period of the prior year; decreased sales volume in our commercial business within our Water Solutions segment driven by the completion of a large project in 2023 that did not recur in the first three months of 2024; and decreased sales volume in our Pool segment primarily due to lower demand compared to the prior year.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Demand is decelerating due to lower sales volumes in multiple segments.

factors.summary: Order backlog is shrinking, particularly in the Pool segment.

evidence.text: The majority of our backlog is short cycle in nature with shipments within one year from when a customer places an order and a substantial portion of our revenues has historically resulted from orders received and products delivered in the same month. A portion of our backlog, particularly from orders for major capital projects, can take more than one year from order to delivery depending on the size and type of order. We record, as part of our backlog, all orders from external customers, which represent firm commitments, and are supported by a purchase order or other legitimate contract. Our backlog of orders is dependent upon when customers place orders and is not necessarily an indicator of our expected results for our 2024 net sales. The decrease in our overall backlog from December 31, 2023 was primarily driven by backlog in our Flow and Pool segments trending down to more historical levels as a result of increased manufacturing capacity and improved lead times.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The backlog is shrinking, particularly in the Pool segment, indicating reduced demand.

factors.summary: The company is successfully raising prices to mitigate inflationary cost increases.

evidence.text: The 1.1 percent decrease in net sales in the first quarter of 2024 from 2023 was partially offset by increased selling prices to mitigate inflationary cost increases.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company is successfully raising prices to mitigate inflationary cost increases.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments, particularly in the Flow and Water Solutions segments.

evidence.text: The 1.9 percent decrease in net sales for Flow in the first quarter of 2024 from 2023 was partially offset by increased sales volume in our commercial flow business compared to the same period of the prior year; and increased selling prices to mitigate inflationary cost increases.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments, which is a positive development.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is decelerating due to lower sales volumes across multiple segments.

evidence.text: The 1.1 percent decrease in net sales in the first quarter of 2024 from 2023 was primarily driven by decreased sales volume in our residential flow business within our Flow segment and our residential business within our Water Solutions segment compared to the same period of the prior year; decreased sales volume in our commercial business within our Water Solutions segment driven by the completion of a large project in 2023 that did not recur in the first three months of 2024; and decreased sales volume in our Pool segment primarily due to lower demand compared to the prior year.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Organic revenue growth is decelerating due to lower sales volumes across multiple segments.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are declining in multiple segments, driven by lower demand.

evidence.text: The 1.1 percent decrease in net sales in the first quarter of 2024 from 2023 was primarily driven by decreased sales volume in our residential flow business within our Flow segment and our residential business within our Water Solutions segment compared to the same period of the prior year; decreased sales volume in our commercial business within our Water Solutions segment driven by the completion of a large project in 2023 that did not recur in the first three months of 2024; and decreased sales volume in our Pool segment primarily due to lower demand compared to the prior year.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Unit volumes are declining in multiple segments, driven by lower demand.

factors.summary: End market momentum is mixed, with the Flow and Water Solutions segments stable, while the Pool segment is slowing.

evidence.text: The 1.9 percent decrease in net sales for Flow in the first quarter of 2024 from 2023 was partially offset by increased sales volume in our commercial flow business compared to the same period of the prior year; and increased selling prices to mitigate inflationary cost increases. The 0.4 percent increase in net sales for Water Solutions in the first quarter of 2024 from 2023 was primarily driven by increased selling prices in our commercial business to mitigate inflationary cost increases. The 1.3 percent decrease in net sales for Pool in the first quarter of 2024 from 2023 was primarily driven by decreased sales volume primarily due to lower demand compared to the prior year.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: End market momentum is mixed, with some segments stable or increasing while others are slowing.

factors.summary: Input costs are rising due to inflationary pressures, particularly in raw materials and labor.

evidence.text: The current volatile market for commodities has the potential to continue to drive price increases in our supply chain. While we have taken pricing actions and implemented transformation initiatives that we expect to improve productivity and offset cost increases, we anticipate supply chain pressures and inflationary cost increases to continue for the remainder of 2024. Inflationary cost increases related to labor costs and certain raw materials.
evidence.subsection: Key Trends and Uncertainties Regarding Our Existing Business

factors.summary: Labor costs are increasing, creating margin pressure.

evidence.text: Inflationary cost increases related to labor costs and certain raw materials.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Labor costs are increasing, creating margin pressure.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are expanding due to pricing actions and productivity improvements.

evidence.text: The 1.3 percentage point increase in gross profit as a percentage of net sales in the first quarter of 2024 from 2023 was primarily driven by increases in selling prices to mitigate impacts of inflationary costs; increased productivity in our Flow and Water Solutions segments mainly driven by manufacturing leverage and transformation initiatives.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Gross and operating margins are expanding due to pricing actions and productivity improvements.

factors.summary: Cost reduction initiatives are delivering measurable savings.

evidence.text: During 2023 and the first three months of 2024, we made strategic progress on our Transformation Program initiatives with a focus on our four key themes of pricing excellence, strategic sourcing, operations excellence and organizational effectiveness. We expect to continue to execute on our key Transformation Program initiatives to drive margin expansion and to continue to incur transformation costs throughout the remainder of 2024 and beyond.
evidence.subsection: Key Trends and Uncertainties Regarding Our Existing Business

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Cost reduction initiatives are delivering measurable savings.

factors.summary: Productivity is improving due to manufacturing leverage and transformation initiatives.

evidence.text: Increased productivity in our Flow and Water Solutions segments mainly driven by manufacturing leverage and transformation initiatives.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Productivity is improving due to manufacturing leverage and transformation initiatives.

factors.summary: Regulatory changes, such as the OECD Pillar Two Model Rules, are impacting the effective tax rate.

evidence.text: The Organization for Economic Co-operation and Development Pillar Two Model Rules ('Pillar Two') for a global 15.0% minimum tax are in the process of being adopted by a number of jurisdictions in which we operate. Pillar Two has negatively impacted our effective tax rate in 2024. That impact could change in the future as we continue to evaluate the enacted legislative changes and as new guidance becomes available.
evidence.subsection: Key Trends and Uncertainties Regarding Our Existing Business

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Regulatory changes are negatively impacting the effective tax rate.

factors.summary: Environmental regulations are not a significant compliance cost or operational constraint.

evidence.text: Not explicitly mentioned in the text.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Environmental regulations are not a significant compliance cost or operational constraint.

factors.summary: Management is confident with actions such as share buybacks and capital expenditures.

evidence.text: We expect to continue to have sufficient cash and borrowing capacity to support working capital needs and capital expenditures, to pay interest and service debt and to pay dividends to shareholders quarterly. We believe our existing liquidity position, coupled with our currently anticipated operating cash flows, will be sufficient to meet our cash needs arising in the ordinary course of business for the next twelve months.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Management is confident with actions such as share buybacks and capital expenditures.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include supply chain pressures and inflationary cost increases.

evidence.text: While we have taken pricing actions and implemented transformation initiatives that we expect to improve productivity and offset cost increases, we anticipate supply chain pressures and inflationary cost increases to continue for the remainder of 2024.
evidence.subsection: Key Trends and Uncertainties Regarding Our Existing Business

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Near-term risks include supply chain pressures and inflationary cost increases.

factors.summary: The company's long-term strategic direction is clear, focusing on ESG and digital innovation.

evidence.text: Our operating objectives focus on delivering our core and building our future. We expect to execute these objectives by: focusing growth initiatives that accelerate our investments in digital, innovation, technology and ESG.
evidence.subsection: Key Trends and Uncertainties Regarding Our Existing Business

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company is focusing on ESG and digital innovation, indicating a positive strategic direction.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include commodity prices, foreign exchange rates, and geopolitical factors.

evidence.text: These factors include the overall global economic and business conditions impacting our business, including the strength of housing and related markets and conditions relating to international hostilities; supply, demand, logistics, competition and pricing pressures related to and in the markets we serve; the ability to achieve the benefits of our restructuring plans, cost reduction initiatives and Transformation Program; the impact of raw material, logistics and labor costs and other inflation; volatility in currency exchange rates and interest rates; failure of markets to accept new product introductions and enhancements; the ability to successfully identify, finance, complete and integrate acquisitions; risks associated with operating foreign businesses; the impact of seasonality of sales and weather conditions; our ability to comply with laws and regulations; the impact of changes in laws, regulations and administrative policy, including those that limit U.S. tax benefits or impact trade agreements and tariffs; the outcome of litigation and governmental proceedings; and the ability to achieve our long-term strategic operating and environmental, social and governance ('ESG') goals and targets.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Multiple risks including economic conditions and geopolitical factors pose challenges.

factors.summary: Catalysts include the ongoing Transformation Program and new product launches.

evidence.text: We have identified specific product and geographic market opportunities that we find attractive and continue to pursue, both within and outside the U.S. We expect to continue investing in our businesses to drive these opportunities through research and development and additional sales and marketing resources.
evidence.subsection: Key Trends and Uncertainties Regarding Our Existing Business

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Ongoing programs and product launches suggest growth opportunities.

factors.summary: Market share is stable with some gains in commercial segments.

evidence.text: The 0.4 percent increase in net sales for Water Solutions in the first quarter of 2024 from 2023 was primarily driven by increased selling prices in our commercial business to mitigate inflationary cost increases.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Stable market share with gains indicates competitive strength.

factors.summary: The company's competitive advantages include technology and sustainability.

evidence.text: At Pentair, we believe the health of our world depends on reliable access to clean water. We deliver a comprehensive range of smart, sustainable water solutions to homes, businesses and industries around the world. Our industry-leading and proven portfolio of solutions enables our customers to access clean, safe water; reduce water consumption; and recover and reuse water.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Technology and sustainability provide significant competitive advantages.

factors.summary: R&D investment is increasing to drive innovation.

evidence.text: We expect to continue investing in our businesses to drive these opportunities through research and development and additional sales and marketing resources.
evidence.subsection: Key Trends and Uncertainties Regarding Our Existing Business

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Increased R&D investment drives innovation and future growth.

evidence.text: The majority of our backlog is short cycle in nature with shipments within one year from when a customer places an order and a substantial portion of our revenues has historically resulted from orders received and products delivered in the same month. A portion of our backlog, particularly from orders for major capital projects, can take more than one year from order to delivery depending on the size and type of order.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Improving supply chain health supports operational efficiency.

evidence.text: Increased productivity in our Flow and Water Solutions segments mainly driven by manufacturing leverage and transformation initiatives.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Increasing capacity utilization indicates productivity gains.

factors.summary: Inventory levels are normalizing.

evidence.text: Not explicitly mentioned in the text.

factors.summary: CapEx is increasing to support growth initiatives.

evidence.text: We expect to continue to have sufficient cash and borrowing capacity to support working capital needs and capital expenditures, to pay interest and service debt and to pay dividends to shareholders quarterly.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Increasing CapEx suggests investment in growth, supported by sufficient cash and borrowing capacity.

factors.summary: Debt leverage is improving with lower net interest expense.

evidence.text: The 15.7 percent decrease in net interest expense in the first quarter of 2024 from 2023 was primarily driven by lower variable debt compared to the same period of the prior year.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Improvement in debt leverage with a significant decrease in net interest expense.

factors.summary: Shareholder returns are maintained through dividends and share repurchases.

evidence.text: Committing to maintain our investment grade rating; focusing on reducing our long-term debt; returning cash to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases; and accelerating our performance with strategically aligned mergers and acquisitions.
evidence.subsection: Key Trends and Uncertainties Regarding Our Existing Business

sentiment.label: positive

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is improving.

evidence.text: We expect to continue to have sufficient cash and borrowing capacity to support working capital needs and capital expenditures, to pay interest and service debt and to pay dividends to shareholders quarterly.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Improving free cash flow, supported by sufficient cash for various needs, including dividends.

factors.summary: Economic conditions are a headwind due to global economic slowdowns.

evidence.text: These factors include the overall global economic and business conditions impacting our business, including the strength of housing and related markets and conditions relating to international hostilities; supply, demand, logistics, competition and pricing pressures related to and in the markets we serve;

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Global economic slowdowns and other factors are acting as headwinds.

factors.summary: Interest rate changes are benefiting the company through lower borrowing costs.

evidence.text: The 15.7 percent decrease in net interest expense in the first quarter of 2024 from 2023 was primarily driven by lower variable debt compared to the same period of the prior year. This decrease was partially offset by increased variable interest rates compared to the same period of the prior year.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Lower borrowing costs despite higher rates, leading to decreased net interest expense.

factors.summary: Foreign exchange movements are impacting margins, particularly in the Pool segment.

evidence.text: The 1.1 percentage point decrease in segment income for Pool as a percentage of net sales in the first quarter of 2024 from 2023 was primarily driven by decreased productivity due to decreased sales volume and investments to drive future growth; and inflationary cost increases related to labor costs and certain raw materials. This decrease was partially offset by increased selling prices to mitigate impacts of inflation.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Negative impact on margins, particularly in the Pool segment, due to foreign exchange movements.
